spec_match.rationale: The lab now names and probes the event-driven handler attributes, GamepadButtonEvent/GamepadAxisEvent surfaces, GamepadEvent baseline, and the navigator.getGamepads() fallback contract from the ChromeStatus feature metadata.

interactive.rationale: A synthetic input harness lets users drive native-event, rAF fallback, and no-API paths through connect, disconnect, button down/up/change, axis move, raw packet, mapping, pressure, axis, and deadzone controls without requiring physical hardware.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The lede and feature-index promise now match the page: feature detection, live gamepad status, event logging, and the polling fallback pattern are all visible.

edge_cases.rationale: The page surfaces unsupported event-driven APIs in Chrome 148, no-gamepad input, no-API fallback, deadzone suppression, analog button changes, and non-standard mapping behavior.

references.rationale: The concept page includes the required chromestatus.com/feature/5989275208253440 link plus the Gamepad specification link.

design_system.rationale: Local styles use shared design tokens for backgrounds, text, borders, accents, and button foreground/background pairs; raw demo color tokens were removed.

summary: Repaired after chrome-devtools-mcp verification on http://localhost:3000/v147/gamepad-event-driven-input-api/compatibility-lab/. Baseline Chrome 148 loaded cleanly, showed navigator.getGamepads() and GamepadEvent support, and correctly reported the event-driven handler attributes and specialized event interfaces as unsupported. The repaired page keeps those exact probes, adds hardware-free controls for all adapter branches and edge cases, and records the unsupported/fallback state rather than treating missing origin-trial support as a crash. The feature conformance route now reports 3 baseline passes and 7 precise event-driven failures in Chrome 148, matching the page's unsupported-state probes.
